location.office_time_required: within commuting distance of LA, SF, or NYC hubs; in-person time for planning, problem-solving, and connection

level_signals: 8+ years of experience in strategy and operations roles
Exceptional leadership and team management skills

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years of experience in strategy and operations roles (eg consulting, finance, GM, Operations)
Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Operations Management, or a related field; MBA preferred
Excellent project management skills, with the ability to handle multiple projects simultaneously
Strong understanding of commerce operations and process optimization
Proficient in data analysis and reporting tools
Exceptional leadership and team management skills
Ability to think strategically and execute methodically
Experience with SQL and analytics tools like Looker, Sigma, Hex

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Operations Management, or a related field; MBA preferred

scope.surface: Post-purchase commerce operations including logistics, payments, user experience, trust and fraud
scope.business_model: marketplace / livestream shopping platform, B2C and seller-facing
core_responsibilities: Develop and implement commerce strategies that drive margin optimization and/or growth across the post-purchase experience
Manage cross-functional commerce projects in close partnership with the product and engineering teams
Identify inefficiencies in processes and implement improvements to enhance productivity/reduce costs
Analyze commerce data to provide insights and recommendations
Negotiate contracts and manage vendor performance to ensure quality and cost-effectiveness

green_flags: Company named #1 Best Startup Employer in America by Forbes
Clear team structure rolling up to VP of Commerce
Remote co-located with flexibility to work from home
Strong benefits package including parental leave, childcare allowance, family planning benefits
red_flags: Must live within commuting distance of LA, SF, or NYC — not fully remote despite 'remote co-located' framing
No equity mention in compensation section despite startup status

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.manages_people (inferred from 'Exceptional leadership and team management skills' and Senior Manager title)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated)
compensation.equity_mentioned (inferred as false; not mentioned in benefits or comp section)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred as false from commuting distance requirement)
ambiguities: JD describes team as 'remote co-located' but also states 'Team members in this role must live within commuting distance of our Los Angeles, San Francisco, or New York City hubs' — treated as hybrid requiring proximity to hub cities, not fully remote.
MBA is listed in the same bullet as the Bachelor's degree requirement ('MBA preferred') — treated as nice-to-have/soft while the Bachelor's is treated as a hard requirement.

Employer types in years_of_experience include 'consulting, finance, GM, Operations' — GM (General Manager) is a role type not employer type, listed in employer_types_named for discoverability but is ambiguous.
